voiceAgentIntegration.title: Voice Agent Integration

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.intro: Mujib AI lets you bring a conversational AI Voice Agent into your business — answering calls, qualifying leads, and handling support requests. These docs cover the three things you need to build an integration: creating secret keys, authenticating your API requests, and connecting the Voice Agent to your business systems.

blocks.text: The Mujib AI API is organized around a few core building blocks that you can combine to fit your workflow:

blocks.desc: used to authenticate every request your server makes to the API.
inbound and outbound calls handled by your configured agent.
real-time events (call started, call ended, ticket created) sent to your backend.

blocks.text: Start with [Secret Keys](/docs/secret-keys) to create your first key, then head to [Authenticating Requests](/docs/authentication) to make your first authenticated call. Once that works, follow [Voice Agent Integration](/docs/voice-agent-integration) to connect the agent to your phone numbers and business systems.
All API requests are made against a single base URL. Every endpoint referenced in these docs is relative to it:

secretKeys.title: Secret Keys
secretKeys.intro: Secret keys authenticate every request your server sends to the Mujib AI API. They are created and managed from your dashboard under `Settings → API Keys`.

blocks.text: Go to `Dashboard → API Keys`.
Click `Create secret key` and give it a descriptive name (e.g. `production-backend`).
Copy the key immediately — for security reasons, the full value is only shown once.
Store it in your environment configuration, not in code.

blocks.title: Key prefix
blocks.text: Secret keys start with `mjb_live_` followed by a unique identifier.

blocks.desc: do not commit secret keys to source control or include them in client-side (browser or mobile) code.
store keys in environment variables or a secrets manager, for example `.env` files excluded from git, or a vault service.

blocks.term: Call the API from your backend only
blocks.desc: only call the Mujib AI API from your backend — proxy any client-facing feature through your own server.
use separate keys per environment (development, staging, production) so you can revoke one without affecting others.

blocks.text: If a key is ever exposed, revoke it immediately from `Dashboard → API Keys` and create a replacement. Requests made with a revoked key will fail with a `401 Unauthorized` response.

blocks.title: Rotate keys periodically
blocks.text: Even without a known leak, rotate production secret keys on a regular schedule, for example every 90 days, as a defense-in-depth practice.

authentication.title: Authenticating Requests
authentication.intro: Every request to the Mujib AI API must include your secret key in the `Authorization` request header, using the `Bearer` scheme.

blocks.text: Requests without a valid header, or with a revoked or malformed key, receive a `401 Unauthorized` response.
Here is a minimal request that lists your Voice Agents, shown in a few common languages:

tabs.label: Node.js
Python
tabs.code:
import os
import requests

response = requests.get(
    "https://api.mujibai.net/v1/agents",
    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {os.environ['MUJIBAI_SECRET_KEY']}"},
)

agents = response.json()

blocks.text: Successful responses return JSON with a `data` field. Errors return a consistent shape you can branch on:

blocks.title: Server-side only
blocks.text: Only send the `Authorization` header from trusted server environments. Never expose your secret key in a browser, mobile app, or any client-side network request.

blocks.title: Use HTTPS
blocks.text: All requests must be made over HTTPS. Plain HTTP requests are rejected.

voiceAgentIntegration.title: Voice Agent Integration
voiceAgentIntegration.intro: The AI Voice Agent can answer inbound calls, place outbound calls, and hand off conversations to your team when needed. This guide walks through connecting an agent to your business.

blocks.text: From `Dashboard → Voice Script`, create an agent and define its script: greeting, business hours, and escalation rules. Each agent has a unique `agent_id`.
Attach a phone number to the agent, either a number you already own (via SIP trunking) or a number provisioned through Mujib AI. This is done from `Dashboard → Settings → Numbers`.
Use the API to have an agent place an outbound call, for example to follow up on a lead:
Configure a webhook endpoint in `Dashboard → Settings → Webhooks` to receive real-time events as calls progress:

blocks.desc: the agent has connected with the caller.
the call has finished, with a transcript and summary.
the agent escalated the call to a support ticket.

blocks.title: Verify webhook signatures
blocks.text: Every webhook request includes an `X-Mujibai-Signature` header. Verify it against your webhook signing secret before trusting the payload.
Test the flow end-to-end with a real call.
Review call transcripts and tune the agent script.
Roll out the integration to your customers when you are ready to launch.
